assertions.reason: Blocked by prerequisite UI interaction failure in browser validation: despite repeated canvas clicks on '新的旅程' and class selection targets, the session never reliably progressed from the menu into a controllable gameplay state, so the dungeon portal and multi-run procedural floor generation could not be exercised through the user surface.

Blocked by the same prerequisite browser UX issue preventing entry into gameplay; unable to reach dungeon floors to compare floor-1 vs floor-5 monster scaling, floor monster variety, or applied difficulty modifiers through the real user surface.
Blocked by inability to traverse from menu into a playable zone in browser validation, so dungeon boss encounters and guaranteed boss loot could not be validated via the real UI flow.
Blocked by prerequisite browser interaction failure before dungeon entry; could not verify exiting/re-entering, fresh run generation, or save/reload return-to-entrance behavior from the user surface.

Blocked because the required progression prerequisite (clearing Normal) could not be achieved from a fresh browser session due to the same menu-to-game interaction problem; therefore Nightmare/Hell unlock sequencing and persistence could not be confirmed in-browser.
Blocked by inability to enter a live run and compare the same monster across Normal, Nightmare, and Hell through the browser surface; no in-surface combat state was reached for measurement.

frictions: Canvas-only Phaser UI was opaque to DOM/a11y tools, requiring coordinate clicks.

Repeated coordinate clicks on the fresh menu and class-selection screens did not yield a reliable transition into a playable zone, making the assigned endgame assertions untestable through the user surface.
Phaser runtime instance was not exposed on window/Phaser.GAMES in this browser context, limiting eval-based state inspection and preventing minimal setup acceleration suggested by the guidance.
blockers: Prerequisite browser UX blocker: from a fresh session at http://localhost:5173/abyssfire/, the validator could reach the class-selection screen but could not reliably transition from menu/class-selection into an interactive gameplay zone, so all dungeon/difficulty assertions remained blocked at the user-surface entry step.
summary: Attempted browser validation for VAL-END-001 through VAL-END-006 from a fresh session, captured evidence screenshots, and confirmed the work is blocked by a prerequisite user-surface interaction issue preventing reliable transition from the menu/class-selection flow into gameplay. No assigned assertion could be positively executed past that prerequisite in the browser.
